name: Ricotta-Sour Cream Cheesecake with Strawberry
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Kiwi fruit, pared and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F. Spray an 8-inch springform pan with non-stick cooking spray.
2. In a large bowl, beat ricotta cheese smooth with an electric mixer. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in sour cream, honey, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, and lemon peel until combined.
3. Pour the mixture into the prepared springform pan. Place the pan in a larger baking pan filled with hot water so it comes halfway up the sides of the pan. Bake for 1 hour or until firm to the touch. Cool to room temperature.
4. Set aside 5 whole strawberries for garnish; slice the remaining berries. In a blender, puree sliced strawberries, sugar, and remaining tablespoon lemon juice until smooth. Strain through cheesecloth into a small bowl.
5. Carefully remove sides of springform pan from cheesecake. Garnish with sliced kiwi fruit and halved strawberries. Serve with strawberry sauce.
Source: Weight Watchers Magazine Light and Easy New Family Classics 1993
Each serving provides: 1 1/2 proteins, 1/4 fruit, 60 optional calories Per serving: 192 calories; 12 g protein, 9 g fat, 15 g carbohydrates, 241 mg calcium, 133 mg sodium, 113 mg cholesterol, 1 g dietary fiber
